growth_records.strain: UCT mixed SRB consortium (maintained on modified Postgate B); SOB enrichment from SRB reactors
growth_records.medium: Modified Postgate B (MPB) medium, lactate-fed, 1 g/L sulphate

up.method: volumetric sulphate reduction rate (VSRR); turbidimetric BaSO4 precipitation; 66% conversion of 1 g/L feed
volumetric lactate utilisation rate; HPLC; complete lactate utilisation at 30C

sec.method: HPLC; produced via incomplete lactate oxidation (Eq2) and lactate fermentation (Eq1); residual acetate typically >10 mmol/L; per-temperature numeric rate not tabulated in main text; also generated from yeast extract
HPLC; produced by lactate fermentation (3 lactate -> acetate + 2 propionate + HCO3-); numeric rate not tabulated in main text
colorimetric N,N-dimethyl-p-phenylenediamine; product of sulphate reduction, partially re-oxidised to elemental sulphur at biofilm; net rate not tabulated

yields.note: 0.228 mmol lactate / 0.144 mmol sulphate = 1.58 mol lactate per mol sulphate reduced (cf. 2.0 for pure incomplete oxidation Eq2, remainder fermented)

growth_records.cite: Marais, T. S. et al. Effect of temperature change on the performance of the hybrid linear flow channel reactor and its implications on sulphate-reducing and sulphide-oxidising microbial community dynamics. Frontiers in Bioengineering and Biotechnology (2022). DOI:10.3389/fbioe.2022.908463. [PMC9458953]
growth_records.snippet: A decrease in volumetric sulphate reduction rates (2 L lactate-fed: 0.144 to 0.059 mmol/L.h ...) ... corresponded to a decrease in volumetric lactate utilization rate from 0.228 to 0.141 mmol/L.h ... within the 2 ... lactate-fed reactor
growth_records.notes: Mixed prokaryotic consortium (not a single strain); rates are VOLUMETRIC (mmol/L reactor/h), not biomass-specific (no gDW measured), so qS in mmol/gDW/h and mu are NOT derivable. culture_mode set to chemostat as nearest continuous-culture enum but biomass is retained (biofilm+microfibers), so growth_rate!=dilution rate; growth_method=not_determined. pH=7.25 taken as midpoint of stated reactor range 7.0-7.5 (approx). Sulphate feed 1 g/L = 1/96 = 10.42 mmol/L; 66% conversion = 6.88 mmol/L reduced. Yield ratio computed as 0.228/0.144=1.58. Acetate/propionate/sulphide are confirmed products but per-condition numeric rates not in main text (trend only).

growth_records.strain: UCT mixed SRB consortium; SOB enrichment from SRB reactors
growth_records.medium: Modified Postgate B (MPB) medium, lactate-fed, 1 g/L sulphate

up.method: volumetric lactate utilisation rate; HPLC; 73 +/- 14% lactate utilisation at 10C

sec.method: HPLC; decreasing production trend with temperature; numeric not tabulated
HPLC; decreasing production trend with temperature; numeric not tabulated

yields.note: 0.141/0.059 = 2.39 mol lactate per mol sulphate reduced at 10C

growth_records.cite: Marais, T. S. et al. Effect of temperature change on the performance of the hybrid linear flow channel reactor and its implications on sulphate-reducing and sulphide-oxidising microbial community dynamics. Frontiers in Bioengineering and Biotechnology (2022). DOI:10.3389/fbioe.2022.908463. [PMC9458953]
growth_records.snippet: At 10 C, lactate utilisation decreased to 73 +/- 14 ... % in the 2 ... lactate-fed reactor ... volumetric lactate utilization rate from 0.228 to 0.141 mmol/L.h ... sulphate reduction rates (2 L lactate-fed: 0.144 to 0.059 mmol/L.h)
growth_records.notes: Same reactor as record 1 but at 10C endpoint. Volumetric rates only; no biomass, mu/qS not derivable. Sulphate conversion fell 66%->27% and VSRR 0.144->0.059 as T dropped 30->10C. Yield ratio 0.141/0.059=2.39. Below the critical temperature (15C) metabolic activity dropped sharply.

growth_records.strain: UCT mixed SRB consortium; SOB enrichment from SRB reactors
growth_records.medium: Modified Postgate B (MPB) medium, lactate-fed, 1 g/L sulphate

up.method: volumetric lactate utilisation rate; HPLC; complete lactate utilisation at 30C

sec.method: HPLC; produced (incomplete oxidation + fermentation + yeast extract); numeric not tabulated
HPLC; fermentation product; numeric not tabulated

yields.note: 0.228/0.128 = 1.78 mol lactate per mol sulphate reduced at 30C

growth_records.cite: Marais, T. S. et al. Effect of temperature change on the performance of the hybrid linear flow channel reactor and its implications on sulphate-reducing and sulphide-oxidising microbial community dynamics. Frontiers in Bioengineering and Biotechnology (2022). DOI:10.3389/fbioe.2022.908463. [PMC9458953]
growth_records.snippet: a decrease in volumetric sulphate reduction rate was observed ... from 0.128 to 0.042 mmol/L.h in the 8 L lactate-fed reactor ... volumetric lactate utilization rate from ... 0.228 to 0.162 mmol/L.h within the ... 8 L lactate-fed reactor
growth_records.notes: 8 L lactate reactor at 30C. Volumetric rates only; mu/qS not derivable (no biomass). Yield ratio 0.228/0.128=1.78. pH held ~7.0.

growth_records.strain: UCT mixed SRB consortium; SOB enrichment from SRB reactors
growth_records.medium: Modified Postgate B (MPB) medium, lactate-fed, 1 g/L sulphate

up.method: volumetric lactate utilisation rate; HPLC; 71 +/- 16% lactate utilisation at 10C

yields.note: 0.162/0.042 = 3.86 mol lactate per mol sulphate reduced at 10C (more lactate diverted to fermentation as BSR falls)

growth_records.cite: Marais, T. S. et al. Effect of temperature change on the performance of the hybrid linear flow channel reactor and its implications on sulphate-reducing and sulphide-oxidising microbial community dynamics. Frontiers in Bioengineering and Biotechnology (2022). DOI:10.3389/fbioe.2022.908463. [PMC9458953]
growth_records.snippet: from 0.128 to 0.042 mmol/L.h in the 8 L lactate-fed reactor ... At 10 C, lactate utilisation decreased to ... 71 +/- 16% in the ... 8 L lactate-fed reactor ... 0.228 to 0.162 mmol/L.h
growth_records.notes: 8 L lactate reactor at 10C endpoint. Volumetric only; mu not derivable. Sulphate conversion 61%->20% over 30->10C; yield ratio 0.162/0.042=3.86.

growth_records.strain: UCT mixed SRB consortium; SOB enrichment from SRB reactors
growth_records.medium: Modified Postgate B (MPB) medium, acetate-fed, 1 g/L sulphate

up.method: HPLC; net acetate uptake could NOT be accurately determined because acetate is co-produced from the fermentable fraction of yeast extract (residual acetate exceeded inlet feed at 30C)

sec.method: colorimetric; product of acetate-driven sulphate reduction (Eq3: acetate + SO4 -> HS- + 2 HCO3-)

growth_records.cite: Marais, T. S. et al. Effect of temperature change on the performance of the hybrid linear flow channel reactor and its implications on sulphate-reducing and sulphide-oxidising microbial community dynamics. Frontiers in Bioengineering and Biotechnology (2022). DOI:10.3389/fbioe.2022.908463. [PMC9458953]
growth_records.snippet: from 0.127 to 0.010 mmol/L.h in the 2 L acetate-fed reactor ... a sulphate conversion of 61% and 54% was achieved at 30 and 25 C, respectively
growth_records.notes: 2 L acetate reactor at 30C. Acetate uptake not quantifiable (acetate also generated from yeast extract), so only sulphate reduction rate is a clean measured uptake. Volumetric only; mu not derivable. pH=7.6 as midpoint of stated 7.2-8.0 range.

growth_records.strain: UCT mixed SRB consortium; SOB enrichment from SRB reactors
growth_records.medium: Modified Postgate B (MPB) medium, acetate-fed, 1 g/L sulphate

up.method: VSRR; turbidimetric BaSO4; only 5% conversion at 10C (4-5x lower than lactate reactors)
HPLC; net uptake not accurately determinable (acetate co-produced from yeast extract)

growth_records.cite: Marais, T. S. et al. Effect of temperature change on the performance of the hybrid linear flow channel reactor and its implications on sulphate-reducing and sulphide-oxidising microbial community dynamics. Frontiers in Bioengineering and Biotechnology (2022). DOI:10.3389/fbioe.2022.908463. [PMC9458953]
growth_records.snippet: In the acetate-fed reactor, operation at 10 C led to a considerable decrease in sulphate reduction activity, only achieving a conversion of 5% ... 0.127 to 0.010 mmol/L.h in the 2 L acetate-fed reactor
growth_records.notes: 2 L acetate reactor at 10C endpoint - near-collapse of BSR (VSRR 0.010 mmol/L.h, 5% conversion). Acetate-oxidising SRB most cold-sensitive of the three systems. Volumetric only; mu not derivable.
